dish_name: Spicy Peanut Noodles with Zucchini and Tomatoes
description: A super easy and tasty take on spicy peanut noodles with zucchini and tomatoes, featuring a luscious peanut sauce with an Asian spin.
ingredients: [{"name": "linguine or spaghetti", "unit": "ounces", "amount": "10 to 12"}, {"name": "zucchini", "unit": null, "amount": "1 medium or 2 small"}, {"name": "tomatoes", "unit": null, "amount": "2 medium"}, {"name": "scallions", "unit": null, "amount": "2 to 3"}, {"name": "bottled Thai peanut sauce or Coconut Peanut Sauce", "unit": "cup", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "hot sauce such as Sriracha", "unit": "teaspoon", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "Salt and freshly ground pepper", "unit": null, "amount": null}, {"name": "Fresh cilantro leaves for garnish", "unit": null, "amount": null}, {"name": "Peanut halves or chopped peanuts for garnish", "unit": null, "amount": null}]
instructions: Cook the pasta according to package directions until al dente, then drain. Combine the zucchini, tomatoes, and scallions with a small amount of water in a medium skillet. Cover and steam until the zucchini is just tender-crisp and the tomato has softened, about 3 to 4 minutes. Combine the cooked noodles with the peanut sauce in a large serving bowl and stir until evenly coated. Add enough hot sauce to please your palate, but don’t go overboard — you can always pass it around to let everyone add more to their own taste. Season with salt and pepper. Top each serving with some of the zucchini-tomato mixture. Garnish each serving with cilantro and/or peanuts, if using; pass around any extra peanut sauce that remains.
notes: You can use bottled peanut satay sauce or make your own Coconut-Peanut Sauce. Substitute your favorite pasta shape if desired. Goes well with tofu or tempeh preparations and raw vegetables.
tags: vegan, spicy, peanut sauce, noodles, zucchini, tomatoes, main course
